title: Elytra Trims
description: Customizable elytra mod with trims, banner patterns and more!
body:
Elytra Trims

With this mod, you can add armor trims, dye your elytra in any color, and put banner patterns on them.


## Installation > [!IMPORTANT] > Mod functionality and compatibility may be different across Minecraft updates! ### Dependencies Elytra Trims requires [Fabric Language Kotlin](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-language-kotlin) or [Kotlin Lang Forge](https://modrinth.com/mod/kotlin-lang-forge) *(alternatively [Kotlin for Forge](https://modrinth.com/mod/kotlin-for-forge))* on the respective mod loader. On 1.20 and 1.21 you may also need [YACL](https://modrinth.com/mod/yacl) and [ModMenu](https://modrinth.com/mod/modmenu) *(Fabric only)*. ### Prerequisites In multiplayer, Elytra Trims **must be installed on the server**, however this **doesn't prevent players without the mod from joining.** | *Elytra Trims = ET* | Player doesn't have ET | Player has ET | |------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------| | Server doesn't have ET | Vanilla experience. | Player can join, but can't apply trims, patterns, etc. to elytras. | | Server has ET | Player can join, but doesn't see trims, patterns, etc. | Player can craft and see decorated elytras. | ## Features ### Trims Trims can be applied to elytras similar to the armor. By default, the mod provides textures for a all vanilla trims, and some custom ones! **Note**: on 1.20 and 1.21 you may need to install [Elytra Trims Extensions](https://modrinth.com/mod/elytra-trims-extensions) for trim compat. ![A few trims](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/XpzGz7KD/images/2966103eb5efb4e408088dbd502da364babf636d.png) Additionally, you can replace the trim pattern in the recipe with a trial key to get access to even more elytras. **Note**: this feature is currently only available on 1.21.10 and newer. ![All the trims](https://i.imgur.com/Wkt5nSa.jpeg) ### Banner patterns Banners can be applied to elytras to show the patterns. In the smithing table, combining elytra with a banner and either paper or leather will transfer banner or shield patterns respectively. **Note**: the patterns are applied **without the base** - you need to dye the elytra to add it. ![Elytra with banner patterns](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/XpzGz7KD/images/0327378aa65018c5f4e761c09c14fa94bb7e1d8d.png) ### Dyeing If you fancy something simplistic, dye your elytra however you want in a crafting table. ![Dyed elytra](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/XpzGz7KD/images/511e985762a518992f2bc140b34e9f5c104ade74.png) ### Glowing Combine an elytra with a glow ink sac in the crafting table to make it glow! ![Glowing trim](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/XpzGz7KD/images/849d54736e4519df78678bf00a2077a528e866ad.png) ### Cosmic effect An expensive endeavor, but if you're willing to part with a nether star, you can apply a unique effect to your elytra. ![Cosmic elytra](https://i.imgur.com/mlISKvX.png) ### Easter eggs Elytra Trims has a couple secret recipes and interactions, which are best discovered by accident, so keep your eyes out! ## Recipes You can find the full information about Elytra Trims recipes on the [wiki](https://codeberg.org/KikuGie/elytra-trims/wiki). ## FAQ ### Are there any conflicts with other mods? - Customizable Elytra: overrides elytra renderer entirely. - Kube JS: breaks elytra decoration recipes. - Lunar/Feather Client: breaks elytra rendering. ### I can't put trims/banners/dyes on the elytra! Please check the following criteria: - Elytra Trims is installed on the server with the correct mod loader. - There aren't any conflicting mods installed. - Recipes may differ across the updates, check the mod changelog page. If nothing helps, please make an [issue report](https://codeberg.org/KikuGie/elytra-trims/issues/new?template=.github%2fISSUE_TEMPLATE%2fbug_report.yml). ### I can't see the trims! Please check the following criteria: - You have Elytra Trims installed on the client with the correct mod loader. - There aren't any conflicting mods installed. If it's a trim provided by a mod or a datapack, it will need custom textures for the elytra. Please make a [trim request](https://codeberg.org/KikuGie/elytra-trims/issues/new?template=.github%2fISSUE_TEMPLATE%2ftrim_request.yml), but be patient, as it takes creative work to make good trim designs. If it's a non-vanilla elytra, it may need explicit support from Elytra Trims. In this or other cases, please make an [issue report](https://codeberg.org/KikuGie/elytra-trims/issues/new?template=.github%2fISSUE_TEMPLATE%2fbug_report.yml).
